mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns in periods of rising industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns in periods of rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening supply environment that reduces new construction and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns in periods of declining housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns in periods of rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns in periods of rising real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns in periods of declining initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns in periods of rising consumer price index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production and a 2-month increase in real disposable personal income signal a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns in periods of rising industrial production and real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
